prerequisites: An eBay seller account whose order and fulfillment data you want to sync.
Membership in the eBay Developers Program with an application keyset (App ID / Client ID and Cert ID / Client Secret) created on the Application Keys page for the Production environment.
An eBay redirect URL (RuName) configured for your application, used as the redirect_uri during user consent.
A completed OAuth user-consent (authorization code grant) flow that yields a long-lived user refresh token for the seller account.
Values for the required config fields: api_host, username, password, redirect_uri, refresh_token, refresh_token_endpoint, and start_date.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 user access token via refresh token
authMethods.summary: eBay RESTful APIs use OAuth 2.0. The Fulfillment API operates on a seller's orders and requires a user access token obtained through the authorization code grant flow (one-time user consent). The connector stores the long-lived user refresh_token (secret) and exchanges it at the refresh_token_endpoint for short-lived user access tokens. The exchange authenticates with your application credentials, supplied here as username (App ID / Client ID) and password (Cert ID / Client Secret, secret), which eBay expects Base64-encoded in the Authorization header.

setupSteps.title: Join the eBay Developers Program and create an application keyset
setupSteps.body: Register for the eBay Developers Program and open the Application Keys page. Generate or copy the Production keyset, which provides your App ID (Client ID) and Cert ID (Client Secret). Use the App ID as the connector username and the Cert ID as the connector password.

setupSteps.title: Configure a redirect URL (RuName)
setupSteps.body: In your eBay developer account, create and configure an eBay redirect URL (RuName) for your application. Provide its value as the connector redirect_uri. This is the redirect target used when a seller grants consent during the OAuth flow.

setupSteps.title: Grant user consent and obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth authorization code grant flow so the eBay seller grants your application the permissions it needs (user consent). Capture the resulting long-lived user refresh token and set it as the connector refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Set the token endpoint and API host
setupSteps.body: Provide eBay's OAuth token endpoint as refresh_token_endpoint; the connector calls it to exchange the refresh_token for a fresh user access token. Set api_host to the eBay API host the connector should call.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync start date
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest date from which orders and fulfillment records should be synced. Records before this date are not retrieved.

sources.title: eBay Authorization guide (authorization code grant, refresh tokens, scopes)
Getting your OAuth credentials (App ID / Cert ID on the Application Keys page)
eBay Access token types (user token and user consent)
eBay Sell Fulfillment API (Developers Program API guide)
